build_spigot, update_readme.

commit.message:
fix(keymaps): guard nil changes in find_hunk_at_cursor

find_hunk_at_cursor() crashed with 'attempt to get length of field
changes (a nil value)' when stored_diff_result existed but its
.changes field was nil (e.g. after single-pane views or during
async transitions).

Add a nil check before accessing #diff_result.changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v1.0.11: interactive mic picker in --setup, guard non-TTY hang, Windows audio fix

- copilot+ --setup now lists all detected audio input devices and lets the
  user pick interactively; chosen device is saved to config automatically
- Guard rl.question behind process.stdin.isTTY so non-interactive callers
  (Homebrew test, CI pipes) never hang
- config.js: extract parseMicDevicesMac / parseMicDevicesWindows helpers and
  export listMicDevices() so --setup can enumerate devices
- config.js: Windows default audioDevice is now null (not ':0') to avoid a
  bogus avfoundation index on Windows
- voice.js: throw a clear error if audioDevice is not configured
- README: document interactive device picker in --setup; update audioDevice
  config table entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Mermaid parse errors in message-queue and distributed-cache

GitHub Mermaid renderer doesn't support '&' chaining on both sides
of labeled arrows (e.g., 'A & B -->|label| C & D'). Split into
individual edges to fix render failures.

- message-queue.md: fix high-level architecture diagram (line 288)
- distributed-cache.md: fix architecture diagram (line 245)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Oxide v0.1 — reactive Rust frontend framework

Scaffold the initial framework with:

- oxide-core: fine-grained reactive signal system (signals, effects,
  batching, untrack) with thread-local arena runtime
- oxide-macros: view! proc macro with JSX-like syntax, compiling to
  direct DOM API calls
- oxide-dom: lightweight DOM renderer via web-sys/wasm-bindgen
- oxide: facade crate with prelude re-exports
- examples/counter: working counter app (30 KB WASM bundle)

All 5 core reactivity tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: flower glow pulse, girl bouquet arm raised, boy legs closer together

- Enhanced flowerGlow SVG filter (3-layer coloured glow)
- Added flowerPulse CSS keyframes animation on field flowers
- Girl together pose: right arm rotated -42deg (was -22), longer (w44), bouquet raised
- Boy legs: reduced spread (center 60/90 vs 55/95)
- LoveStory bottom bar: music+lang+skip in single flex row for mobile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove 3 non-deterministic HMP commands; add system_reset op; disk guard

- Replace 'info registers', 'info cpu', 'info irq' in HMP_COMMANDS with
  deterministic alternatives ('info lapic', 'info kvm', 'info version').
  These three commands contained CPU pc= values and IRQ timer counts that
  varied with concurrent SeaBIOS execution, causing inflated corpus add rate.
- Add op 16 (system_reset): exercises QEMU machine reset code paths, a known
  source of use-after-free and re-initialization bugs. Live hotplug resources
  survive reset and continue to be exercised afterwards.
- Update ChunkOpMutator to use below(17) to cover all 17 ops uniformly.
- Add 2 new seeds: seed-system-reset.bin and seed-reset-with-device.bin
  (device_add → system_reset → device_del stress sequence).
- Add disk guard to run_fuzzer.sh: wipes queue if sync/ exceeds 10 GB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 10 code review issues (injection, race, safety)

- Escape user input in storage_get/storage_set with json_escape (browser.rs)
- Escape cookie name/value/domain in cookies_set/cookies_clear (browser.rs)
- Escape keys/values in session_import (browser.rs)
- Fix incomplete dialog prompt escaping with json_escape (browser.rs)
- Validate ADB serial for shell meta-characters (android.rs)
- Eliminate TOCTOU race in recording_stop by merging lock scopes (agent.rs)
- Rename misleading parse_yaml to parse_json_compat (workflow.rs)
- Add path traversal guard for SubWorkflow paths (workflow.rs)
- Fix off-by-one: retries now means retry count, not total attempts (workflow.rs)
- Log warning on safety policy mutex contention (server.rs)
- Return error on empty session ID in ios.rs and android.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: onecrawl
repository.description: Native TypeScript web crawler and scraper. Zero Python dependencies.

commit.message:
Enhance 11 concept pages with concrete code examples and proofs

Applied targeted additions identified by review — each fills a gap
where an abstract explanation benefits from concrete illustration.

Code examples added:
- caching.md: Python cache-aside get/set with DB fallback
- message-queues.md: Kafka producer (partition key) + consumer group
- websockets.md: Node.js server (ws + heartbeat) + browser client
- api-gateway.md: Kong declarative YAML (JWT, rate-limit, transformer)
- service-discovery.md: Consul registration + Flask /health endpoint
- fault-tolerance.md: Python retry with full jitter + anti-pattern
- auth.md: Decoded JWT with header/payload/verification steps

Visual proofs added:
- database-sharding.md: Celebrity hotspot example (950K vs 10K req/s)
- replication.md: Quorum R+W>N overlap proof with all read combos
- scalability.md: Resharding — modulo (80% move) vs consistent hash (20%)

Header examples added:
- monitoring.md: W3C traceparent + B3/Zipkin trace context headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): move release.git config to subcommand-level in nx.json (#16)

When using nx release sub-commands (version, changelog, publish)
individually, the top-level 'release.git' property is not allowed.
Git options must be configured under 'release.version.git' and
'release.changelog.git' instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): backfill paddle_customer_id from API response when missing

Both update and cancel routes now check if paddle_customer_id is null
on the user profile after a successful Paddle API call. If missing,
it's populated from the Paddle response's customer_id field.
This ensures the customer portal button works even if the webhook
that originally saves customer_id was missed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial plan (#173)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 20 core system design concept write-ups

New concepts/ directory with 7,252 lines across 20 standalone
concept pages + README index. Each includes Mermaid diagrams,
comparison tables, and cross-references to full design docs.

Fundamentals (5):
- Load Balancing (318 lines) — L4/L7, 8 algorithms, HA diagrams
- Caching (342 lines) — 4 patterns, eviction policies, stampede
- Database Sharding (308 lines) — 5 strategies, resharding
- Replication (365 lines) — single/multi/leaderless, failover
- CAP Theorem (383 lines) — triangle, PACELC, tunable consistency

Communication (5):
- Consistent Hashing (235 lines) — ring, virtual nodes, math
- Message Queues (291 lines) — P2P/pub-sub, delivery guarantees
- WebSockets (304 lines) — vs polling/SSE, scaling, heartbeat
- API Gateway (311 lines) — BFF, middleware chain, data/control plane
- Service Discovery (394 lines) — client/server-side, Consul/K8s

Data & Storage (3):
- Database Indexing (365 lines) — B-tree, LSM, composite, covering
- Data Partitioning (406 lines) — hash/range, rebalancing
- Eventual Consistency (450 lines) — CRDTs, vector clocks, quorum

Architecture (2):
- Microservices (490 lines) — saga pattern, bounded contexts, DDD
- Rate Limiting (594 lines) — 5 algorithms, Redis Lua, hybrid

Reliability (5):
- Scalability (310 lines) — vertical/horizontal, Amdahl's Law
- Fault Tolerance (338 lines) — circuit breaker, chaos engineering
- Monitoring (365 lines) — metrics/logs/traces, RED/USE/Golden
- Auth (393 lines) — OAuth2, JWT, RBAC/ABAC, mTLS
- CDN (257 lines) — pull/push, cache hierarchy, multi-CDN

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
clear_print_error: add uiop dialog-close signal + update behavioral rules

BambuStudio sends two commands when dismissing a print error dialog:
1. clean_print_error - clears the print_error value
2. uiop (system/dialog-close) - signals UI acknowledgment to the printer

Without the uiop signal the printer stays in UI-acknowledgment pending
state, causing any open BambuStudio session to re-raise print_error on
every push_status, overriding the clear.

Changes:
- clear_print_error() now calls printer.clean_print_error_uiop() after
  printer.clean_print_error(), using the new BPM method
- Dropped inline send_anything() + import json (replaced by BPM method)
- Updated docstring to document the two-command protocol
- behavioral_rules: added two-command clear protocol rule and
  gcode_state FAILED semantics after cancellation

Companion BPM change: adds CLEAN_PRINT_ERROR_UIOP_TEMPLATE, the
clean_print_error_uiop() method, and restores toJson() as a proper
standalone method (fixing a pre-existing regression from the
clean_print_error commit).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: On-device conversational search (RAG) — unified natural language & keyword search (#244)

* Initial plan

* chore: start RAG assistant implementation

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add on-device RAG conversational reference assistant

- Add ragSearch utility with TF-IDF scoring over all reference entries
- Add RagAssistantScreen with chat-style UI, result cards, and jump-to-section navigation
- Register RagAssistant route in AppNavigator
- Add chat-bubble icon button to SectionHeader for assistant access
- Add 29 unit tests covering tokenize, scoreEntry, createExcerpt, ragSearch

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: unify Search + RAG into single conversational screen; fix footer overflow

- Rewrite SearchScreen as a unified chat-style screen: tapping the section
  header opens one screen for both keyword queries and natural language questions
- On send, runs RAG (reference content) + keyword search (user data) in parallel
  and renders combined results as conversation messages with "Jump to section" cards
- Fix footer overflow: move paddingBottom: FOOTER_HEIGHT to the outer container,
  outside KeyboardAvoidingView, matching the codebase layout convention
- Revert SectionHeader to single-button (header tap → Search); remove separate
  RAG chat-bubble icon button
- Remove now-unused RagAssistant route and RagAssistantScreen import from AppNavigator

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: move search input to top of screen with section-header styling

The input bar now sits at the top of SearchScreen, in the same position
as the SectionHeader — centered, 80% wide, borderWidth 2, borderRadius 8,
fontSize 20 / fontWeight 800, same SECONDARY_ACCENT + TOAST_BROWN colors.
A HorizontalRule follows the input, matching SectionHeader layout exactly.
The send button appears inline inside the input box only when text has been
entered. Chat message history scrolls below the rule, leaving the input
always visible at the top.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: scroll to user message; expand RAG index to all hardcoded content

Scroll behavior:
- onLayout tracks each message's Y offset in a Map<id, y> ref
- After results arrive, scrollTo(userMsgY - 8) so the question is near
  the top and the user can scroll down through results at their own pace
- Falls back to scrollToEnd if layout isn't recorded yet

RAG index expansion (Morse and all hardcoded modules):
- Import scenarioCards.json (24 entries) via scenarioCardToEntry adapter
  (first_5_minutes/first_hour/first_day → steps; immediate_risks → do_not)
- 5 synthetic ReferenceEntryType entries: Morse Code, NATO Phonetic,
  Radio Frequency References, Ground-to-Air Signals, Digital Whistle
- Each synthetic entry has rich tags/steps for natural language matching
  and a related_screen pointing to its app screen

Smart navigation:
- handleJumpToEntry uses entry.related_screen when present
- Button label uses related_screen_label (e.g. "Open Morse Code")
- SearchScreenNavigationProp extended with all synthetic screen names

Tests: 6 new tests for Morse, Radio Frequencies, Ground-to-Air, scenario
cards, and related_screen correctness (594 total, all passing)

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reorder imports in ragSearch test

Move ReferenceEntryType import before the utility imports in __tests__/ragSearch.test.ts to improve import ordering and consistency (addresses lint/import-order preferences).

* feat: expand RAG index to cover every tool in every module

Every tool screen that didn't previously have a synthetic index entry now
does, so natural language queries like "unit conversion" or "lunar cycles"
return the right result.

New synthetic entries:
- Core: Unit Conversion, Lunar Cycles, Barometric Pressure, Sun Times,
  Device Status, Flashlight, Voice Log
- Navigation: Map, Star Map & Celestial Navigation
- Prepper: Emergency Planner

All entries have rich tags, descriptive steps, and a related_screen so
the "Open …" button navigates directly to the correct tool screen.

SearchScreen navigation type updated with all new screen names.
8 new tests added (602 total, all passing).

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reformat ragSearch tests to wrap long lines

Reformat __tests__/ragSearch.test.ts to wrap long string arguments and expect expressions across multiple lines for improved readability (line-length / prettier style). No functional changes to test behavior.

* refactor: remove unused RagAssistantScreen

The screen was an orphan — not imported or registered in AppNavigator.
The unified SearchScreen covers all search + RAG functionality.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: cancel pending setTimeout timers when SearchScreen unmounts

Store both setTimeout IDs in a ref and clear them in a useEffect cleanup
to prevent "setState on unmounted component" warnings when the user
navigates away while a search is in progress.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Significantly improve AI chess engine strength

- Create shared chess engine (lib/chess-engine.ts) with:
  - Piece-square tables for all 6 piece types (middlegame + endgame king)
  - Negamax search with alpha-beta pruning
  - Quiescence search (captures + checks, 6 ply deep)
  - MVV-LVA move ordering for better pruning
  - Opening book with 25+ common positions
  - Bishop pair bonus, check bonus, mobility evaluation
  - Endgame detection with king endgame PST
- Increase search depths: Advanced=5, Master=6 (desktop); up to depth 6 (mobile)
- Both mobile and desktop play-ai now use shared engine
- Remove duplicate inline minimax implementations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace MemoryBarrier with Volatile operations in HashMap (#125259)

Replace full `MemoryBarrier()` fences with narrower `VolatileStore`
(release) and `VolatileLoad` (acquire) operations in `hash.cpp`. Full
fences (`dmb ish` on ARM64, `mfence` on x86) are unnecessarily expensive
when only one-directional ordering is needed.

## Changes

- **InsertValue**: `VolatileStore` on key publication (release) ensures
the value is visible before the key.
- **LookupValue/ReplaceValue/DeleteValue**: `VolatileLoad` on key match
(acquire) ensures subsequent value reads are ordered after the key.
- **ReplaceValue**: Plain store the new value, then `VolatileStore` on
the key (release) to re-publish it. Readers acquire-load the key via
`VolatileLoad`, forming a proper release-acquire pair on the same
address and ensuring they observe either the old or the fully-updated
new value.
- **Rehash**: `VolatileStore` on `m_rgBuckets` (release) ensures new
bucket contents are visible before the pointer is published. Readers
observe these writes because they enter an EBR critical region
(`EbrCriticalRegionHolder::EnterCriticalRegion`), which executes a full
`MemoryBarrier()` before reading `m_rgBuckets`.

## Impact on ARM64

| Site | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| InsertValue | `dmb ish` + `str` | `stlr` |
| Reader key match | `dmb ish` | `ldar` |
| ReplaceValue write | `str` + `dmb ish` | `str` + `stlr` (key
re-publish) |
| Rehash pointer publish | `dmb ish` + `str` | `stlr` |

On x86/x64, `MemoryBarrier()` emits `mfence` (or `lock or`), whereas
`VolatileLoad`/`VolatileStore` are just compiler fences (TSO provides
hardware ordering).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
